knowledge.insight: Hackney: 'After bass finish spawning their primary instinct is to feed. Females shift focus to recovery and nourishment quickly after spawning — sometimes over multiple beds. Males remain shallow longer guarding fry'

Hackney: 'Bass are opportunistic feeders. During post-spawn they target dominant forage — on many lakes bluegill begin spawning right after bass, becoming the primary food source. On other lakes it may be shad, blueback herring, or crawfish'
Hackney: 'Check the mouths of caught fish for signs of their recent meals — shad tails, crawfish antennae, or bluegill fins. That detail often leads to the right bait choice'
